title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: Consider the inverting amplifier using an ideal operational amplifier shown in the figure. The designer wishes to make the input resistance seen by the small-signal source as large as possible while keeping the voltage gain between -10 and -25. The upper limit on \(R_F\) is 1 \(M\Omega\). What should the value of \(R_1\) be?

title: Dataset Open Question 1
rewritten_question_text: A copper bar is placed in a uniform magnetic field, with the direction of electron flow in the bar as shown in the image. What electrical effect occurs between points \( a \) and \( b \) on the copper bar?
expected_answer: A small potential difference is generated between points \( a \) and \( b \) on the copper bar.

title: Dataset Open Question 2
rewritten_question_text: A copper bar is placed in a uniform magnetic field, with the direction of electron flow in the bar as shown in the image. Between points \( a \) and \( b \), which point is at the higher potential?
expected_answer: Point \( a \) is at the higher potential; \( U_a > U_b \).

title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text:
In figure (a), \( r(t) = s(t) + n(t) \), where \( s(t) = m(t) \cos(2 \pi f_c t) - \hat{m}(t) \sin(2 \pi f_c t) \) is the modulated signal with carrier frequency \( f_c = 5 \) kHz. The signal \( m(t) \) is a baseband signal with power 3 W and bandwidth 2 kHz, and \( \hat{m}(t) \) is the Hilbert transform of \( m(t) \). The term \( n(t) \) is narrowband noise with a power spectral density shown in figure (b), where the vertical axis is in W/kHz. The \( \boldsymbol{LPF} \) is an ideal low-pass filter with cutoff frequency 2 kHz.

Calculate the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) at each output, expressed in decibels (dB).
